subsections.text: In establishing pilotage rates, the board shall consider factors relevant to determining reasonable and just pilotage rates, including:
characteristics of vessels to be piloted;
the average number of hours spent by a pilot performing:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: pilot services on board vessels; and
costs to pilots to provide the required pilot services;
the public interest in maintaining safe, efficient, and reliable pilot services;
the average wages of masters of United States flag vessels that navigate in the board’s jurisdiction and for which the pilotage rate is to be established;
economic factors affecting the shipping industry in the area in which the port is located; and
an adequate and reasonable compensation for the pilots and a fair return on the equipment and vessels that the pilots employ in connection with their duties.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
